rows.definition: abi.RegisterCapability is the registry writer that adds a negotiable feature token to the kernel's process-global Supported() capability set, called from a driver's init().
rows.distinction: It registers a negotiable FEATURE FLAG, not a tool-admission rung — unlike RegisterAdjudicator, which inserts a PDP/PEP decision link into the call-folding chain.

rows.definition: abi.RegisterAdjudicator inserts a PDP/PEP adjudicator link into the kernel's pre-call decision chain at a numeric rank (lower rank runs first) and rebuilds the registry snapshot.
rows.distinction: It registers a PRE-CALL reference-monitor rung, not a feature token — unlike RegisterCapability, which only flips a negotiable capability bit.

rows.definition: kernel.WithAdjudicators constructs a kernel that folds an EXPLICIT caller-supplied adjudicator chain instead of the process-global registry, so K replay arms each carry their own monitor without colliding.
rows.distinction: It injects a per-kernel chain for isolated replay, not a global registration — unlike RegisterAdjudicator, which mutates the one process-global registry.

rows.definition: abi.AdjudicatorsFor returns the rung chain a given ToolCall must fold in rank order — the unconditional rungs plus any rung tool-scoped to that call — as the registry's immutable slice.
rows.distinction: It SELECTS the chain to fold for one call, it does not register a rung — unlike RegisterAdjudicator, which adds a rung to the registry.

rows.definition: Server.adjudicateProposed gates every proposed tool call in a buffered upstream turn through the kernel, returning the kept calls, per-call ToolAdjudication records, and a dropped count.
rows.distinction: It is the gateway's BATCH pre-execution gate over a model's proposed calls, not the single-call reference monitor — unlike adjudicator.Adjudicate, the per-call decision function it delegates to.

rows.definition: Server.handleFakAdjudicate is the /v1/fak/adjudicate HTTP handler that returns the pre-execution verdict only — no dispatch, no engine, no pending state — for a client that runs its own tools.
rows.distinction: It returns a CALL verdict before execution, not result containment — unlike handleFakAdmit, the served-path complement that quarantines a tool RESULT after.

rows.definition: gateway.ToolAdjudication is the per-tool-call record of the kernel's decision on one proposed call — its id, tool, admitted bit, WireVerdict, and any repaired arguments.
rows.distinction: It is the per-CALL decision record, not the aggregate — unlike AdjudicationSummary, the run-level counter roll-up folded into the exit summary.

rows.definition: gateway.AdjudicationSummary is the aggregate per-run counter struct (provider token axes, cache reads, decision counts) folded into the guard exit summary.
rows.distinction: It aggregates a whole run's decisions and observed token axes, not one call — unlike ToolAdjudication, the single-call verdict record.

rows.definition: gateway.adjudicationNote renders a short agent-readable in-band summary of a turn's non-trivial decisions (drops + arg repairs) for clients that read only the content channel and never the fak extension.
rows.distinction: It is the human/agent-facing PROSE of the decisions, not the machine counter roll-up — unlike AdjudicationSummary, the numeric aggregate.

rows.definition: gateway FakExt.Adjudications is the JSON field in the gateway's non-standard response extension carrying the ToolAdjudication record for every tool_call the model proposed, including the dropped ones.
rows.distinction: It is the WIRE list of per-call decisions returned to the client, not the run-level counters — unlike AdjudicationSummary, the exit-summary aggregate.

rows.definition: Adjudicator.SetPolicy swaps the live decision table, re-indexing arg predicates and clamping (sanitizeProfile) any profile that tries to drop a mandatory write-class refusal rung.
rows.distinction: It mutates the running monitor's policy in memory, not a manifest on disk — unlike Manifest.ToPolicy, which compiles a manifest into a Policy value.

rows.definition: adjudicator.DefaultPolicy is the v0.1 baseline decision table — allow the read-only tool family, fail-closed otherwise — used when no manifest is loaded.
rows.distinction: It is the built-in fallback table, not an operator's loaded rules — unlike a Manifest compiled via ToPolicy, which an operator authors.

rows.definition: policy.Manifest.ToPolicy validates a manifest (version + closed-vocabulary deny reasons) and compiles it into the runtime adjudicator.Policy.
rows.distinction: It COMPILES a parsed manifest into a runtime Policy, it does not install it — unlike SetPolicy, which swaps a compiled Policy onto the live monitor.

rows.definition: cmd/fak applyPolicy loads a capability-floor manifest from the --policy path and installs it onto the process-global adjudicator.Default (plus its runtime side-effects) at startup.
rows.distinction: It is the one-shot CLI install of a manifest at boot, not the live in-place reload — unlike policyReloader, which rebuilds the floor on a running gateway.

rows.definition: cmd/fak policyReloader returns the gateway PolicyReloadFunc that re-loads the capability-floor manifest in-place on a running gateway (re-SetPolicy + runtime apply) and reports a reload summary.
rows.distinction: It hot-reloads the floor on a live process, not the startup install — unlike applyPolicy, the one-shot load at boot.

rows.definition: turnbench.RunPolicyReplay scores K policy variants against ONE recorded trajectory as model-free kernel replays, emitting per-arm divergence witnesses and the cube-collapse cost accounting.
rows.distinction: It is the same-trace K-policy replay SPINE, not a single variant — unlike PolicyArm, the one policy entry it replays.

rows.definition: turnbench.PolicyArm is one named policy variant in a same-trace comparison — a label plus the adjudicator.Policy swapped onto the monitor for that arm's replay.
rows.distinction: It is a single variant input, not the replay engine — unlike RunPolicyReplay, the spine that folds all arms over the trace.

rows.definition: abi.ReasonPolicyBlock is the closed-vocabulary reason code (POLICY_BLOCK) the kernel cites when an EXPLICIT policy rule denied a call.
rows.distinction: It marks an explicit-rule denial, not the catch-all fail-closed — unlike ReasonDefaultDeny, cited when no rule affirmatively allowed the call.

rows.definition: cachemeta.ReasonPolicyMismatch is the cache-lookup MISS reason ("policy_mismatch") returned when a candidate entry's bound policy version no longer matches the live policy.
rows.distinction: It is a cache-coherence miss reason, not a tool-admission refusal — unlike ReasonPolicyBlock, the adjudicator's POLICY_BLOCK denial code.

rows.definition: cachemeta Validity.PolicyVersion is the policy-version string stamped on a cache entry that bounds its validity, so a policy change invalidates entries minted under the old version.
rows.distinction: It is a cache-entry validity STAMP, not a refusal code — unlike ReasonPolicyMismatch, the miss reason raised when that stamp no longer matches.

rows.definition: cachemeta.InvalidationPolicy is the InvalidationMode constant "policy" — the coherence mode that drops a cache entry when policy changes, distinct from the lru/ttl/write_epoch modes.
rows.distinction: It is a cache INVALIDATION trigger, not the validity stamp it acts on — unlike PolicyVersion, the per-entry string the policy-mode invalidation compares.

rows.definition: cachemeta.LifecyclePolicy parameterizes the time-driven cache-tier transitions — per-tier TTLs, a default TTL, the expiring->expired grace window, and demote-vs-evict on expiry.
rows.distinction: It governs TIME-driven tier aging, not policy-change invalidation — unlike InvalidationPolicy, the coherence mode keyed on policy version.

rows.definition: PolicyEvictions counts explicit EvictNode calls in the radix KV cache — entries dropped by a deliberate policy action — surfaced as fak_gateway_kv_memory_evictions_total{kind="policy"}.
rows.distinction: It counts policy-driven KV-node drops, not budget pressure drops — unlike the plain Evictions counter, the LRU budget evictions.

rows.definition: model.DynamicPrecisionPolicy is an online quantization controller that accepts a Q8_0 decode result only when the logit top1-top2 margin / top1 probability clears a threshold, else recomputes the span in f32.
rows.distinction: It is a numeric-PRECISION acceptance policy for decode, not a tool-admission policy — unlike DefaultPolicy, the adjudicator's tool decision table.

rows.definition: gateway.ResetPolicy holds the context-reset decision thresholds (min turns, healthy/stale read ratios, cooldown) that ResetScore folds to recommend a fresh-context reset over another compaction cut; DefaultResetPolicy supplies the conservative defaults.
rows.distinction: It decides session CONTEXT reset-vs-cut from cache health, not tool admission — unlike DefaultPolicy, the adjudicator floor.

rows.definition: gateway.BatchPolicy holds the dynamic-batching knobs (max batch, max pad overhead, max prompt length) that bound rectangular-prefill batch selection; DefaultBatchPolicy gives the shipping defaults.
rows.distinction: It is a throughput/batching control policy, not a security decision policy — unlike DefaultPolicy, the adjudicator tool floor.

rows.definition: adjudicator.SecretPosture is the policy-author knob selecting what the on-discovery secret rung does when a tool RESULT bears a credential — quarantine (default), fail_closed, or admit_and_log.
rows.distinction: It governs the secret-discovery verdict specifically, not the general default-deny stance — unlike Posture, the tool-admission fail-closed/admit-and-log selector it reuses.

rows.definition: toollint ToolFacts.HasPreflightSchema is the fact-bit true iff the pre-flight ladder (rung-1) enforces a parameter schema for a tool.
rows.distinction: It records whether rung-1 schema validation exists, not whether the policy denies the tool — unlike PolicyDenied, the fact that the adjudicator refuses it by name.

rows.definition: toollint ToolFacts.PolicyDenied is the fact-bit true iff the adjudicator policy provably refuses a tool by name via its Deny map.
rows.distinction: It is a lint FACT about a tool's policy status, not the runtime refusal code — unlike ReasonPolicyBlock, the verdict reason cited at decision time.

rows.definition: swebench.EvalCapability is the probe result describing whether the official grader harness is runnable here — Docker present, swebench importable, the resolved python, and a Runnable bit with a reason.
rows.distinction: It is a BENCH-harness readiness capability, not a negotiated kernel feature token — unlike RegisterCapability's Capability, the ABI feature-flag set.

rows.definition: swebench.DetectEvalCapability probes the box for Docker and an importable swebench harness and returns the populated EvalCapability with a Runnable verdict and reason.
rows.distinction: It is the PROBE that produces an EvalCapability, not the struct itself — unlike EvalCapability, the value it returns.

rows.definition: PolicyBreaches counts the policy-violating actions an arm took during a benchmark task (browser-action / terminal-bench), feeding the SafeSuccess gate.
rows.distinction: It tallies how many violations slipped through in a measured run, not the kernel's per-call denial — unlike ReasonPolicyBlock, the verdict code an individual refusal cites.

rows.definition: PolicyBreachRate is the arm-level ratio of policy breaches to total actions/commands in a benchmark summary.
rows.distinction: It is the normalized RATE, not the raw tally — unlike PolicyBreaches, the integer count it divides.

rows.definition: PolicyBreachDelta is the raw-arm-minus-fak-arm difference in policy breaches, the head-to-head measure of how many violations the fak floor prevented.
rows.distinction: It is the between-arm difference (raw vs fak), not one arm's count or rate — unlike PolicyBreaches and PolicyBreachRate, the per-arm tally and ratio.
